summary: This rate is based on a small number of injuries (21 to 29 per year) divided by ridership in the millions, so it is sensitive to small year-to-year changes. DOT attributed the Fiscal 2023 rise to 1.97 injuries per million passengers to a rebound in ridership as the ferry returned to normal post-pandemic service, with slip-and-fall incidents as the leading cause; the number of injuries then declined even as ridership kept growing, improving the rate to 1.46 by Fiscal 2025.
